topic: GLM-5.1 Review 2026: #1 SWE-bench Pro, MIT License, $1/M Tokens

key_points: GLM-5.1 is #1 open source AI model on SWE-Bench Pro with 58.4 score
754B MoE architecture with 40B active parameters
MIT license allows commercial use and fine-tuning
API pricing starts at $1.40/M input tokens
First open-weight model to top SWE-Bench Pro
Surpasses GPT-5.4 (57.7) and Claude Opus 4.6 (57.3) on SWE-Bench Pro
Released April 7, 2026 by Z.AI (formerly Zhipu AI)
Up to 8 hours of continuous autonomous task execution
DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA) for efficient long-context handling
Asynchronous reinforcement learning via Z.AI slime framework
203K context window
Available on Hugging Face and Ollama
Matches GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus on standard coding benchmarks
Open-weight model with commercial use permitted under MIT license
Drop-in replacement for OpenAI SDK via vLLM OpenAI-compatible API
Self-hosting possible with quantized GGUF versions
Open-source model beating proprietary closed models on SWE-Bench Pro
Represents a major milestone for open-weight frontier models
Cost advantage vs Claude Opus 4.7 at $1.40/$4.40 per 1M vs $5/$25
Autonomous experiment-analyze-optimize loop in long-horizon tasks

stats.fact: GLM-5.1 achieves 58.4 on SWE-Bench Pro, #1 globally as of April 2026
GLM-5.1 surpasses GPT-5.4 (57.7) and Claude Opus 4.6 (57.3) on SWE-Bench Pro
API pricing: $1.40/M input tokens, $4.40/M output tokens, $0.26/M cached input tokens
Claude Opus 4.7 priced at $5.00/$25.00 per 1M tokens vs GLM-5.1 at $1.40/$4.40
GLM-5.1 can autonomously work on a single task for up to 8 hours
Ranks #14 out of 115 models on the BenchLM provisional leaderboard (overall score 83/100)
At 77.8% SWE-bench Verified, only 3 points behind Claude Opus 4.6 (80.8%) and GPT-5.2 (80.0%)
FP8 quantization requires 860GB VRAM minimum; 8xH200 GPUs recommended
1-bit GGUF quantization (176GB) runs on ~180GB system RAM without GPU via llama.cpp
GLM-5.1 completed 655 autonomous iterations optimizing a vector database, achieving 6.9x throughput improvement

angles: First open-weight model to claim #1 on SWE-Bench Pro — a watershed moment for open-source AI
Cost disruption: GLM-5.1 at $1.40/M input vs Claude Opus 4.7 at $5.00/M — 3.5x cheaper for comparable coding quality
MIT license vs restricted proprietary licenses: what it means for enterprises and startups
8-hour autonomous coding sessions: the new frontier for agentic software engineering
Self-hosting viability: can teams actually run a 754B model on-premises with quantization?
China open-source AI surge: GLM-5.1 follows DeepSeek in challenging US AI dominance
SWE-Bench Pro caveat: partial self-reported scores and independent verification gap
GLM-5.1 for production: when to choose it over Claude or GPT for coding workloads
